pyensemblefs
  • API Reference
  • Feature Selection Methods
  • View page source

Feature Selection Methods

The module pyensemblefs.fsmethods implements stand–alone feature selection algorithms that can be used directly or combined with the ensemble mechanisms provided in pyensemblefs.ensemble.

All methods inherit from pyensemblefs.fsmethods.basefs.FSMethod, which standardizes:

  • selected_features_ – binary mask

  • ranking_ – rank array (1 = most important)

  • feature_importances_ – raw relevance scores

  • target_type – "classification" or "regression"

Classification of Methods

pyensemblefs organizes feature selection algorithms into three functional groups:

  • Subset filters → directly output a set of selected features.

  • Ranking filters → produce a full ordering of features.

  • Score-based filters → compute a scoring function, which is then converted into a ranking or subset.

This structure mirrors the taxonomy used in the scientific manuscript, allowing consistent aggregation and stability analysis across methods.

Subset Methods

Subset-based methods return a binary mask indicating which features belong to the selected subset.

SubsetFilter

Rule-based subset selector.

Key parameters: - rule={"variance"} - k – number of features to select - threshold – minimum variance (when k is not specified)

Typical use: .. code-block:: python

from pyensemblefs.fsmethods import SubsetFilter fs = SubsetFilter(rule=”variance”, k=10) fs.fit(X, y)

MRMRSubset

Minimum Redundancy – Maximum Relevance (mRMR) selector.

Characteristics: - Relevance: mutual information (MI) - Redundancy: MI-based or correlation-based - Greedy forward selection

Parameters: - k – number of features - redundancy={"mi","corr"} - discrete_features – handling of categorical variables

Example: .. code-block:: python

from pyensemblefs.fsmethods import MRMRSubset fs = MRMRSubset(k=8, redundancy=”mi”) fs.fit(X, y)

CFSSubset

Correlation-based Feature Selection (CFS).

Merit function: - Maximize relevance to target - Minimize redundancy among selected features

Example: .. code-block:: python

from pyensemblefs.fsmethods import CFSSubset fs = CFSSubset(k=12) fs.fit(X, y)

FCBFSubset

Fast Correlation-Based Filter (FCBF).

Procedure: 1. Discretize features (quantile-based binning). 2. Compute Symmetrical Uncertainty (SU) w.r.t. the target. 3. Eliminate redundant features based on SU thresholds.

Parameters: - delta – minimum SU - n_bins – discretization bins - k – optional maximum number of features

Example: .. code-block:: python

from pyensemblefs.fsmethods import FCBFSubset fs = FCBFSubset(delta=0.05, n_bins=5) fs.fit(X, y)

Ranking Methods

Ranking-based methods return a complete ordering of features.

RankingFilter

Generic ranking selector used internally by score-based and simple ranking rules.

Provided scorers include: - "variance" - custom scorers implemented by the user

Used as:

from pyensemblefs.fsmethods import RankingFilter
fs = RankingFilter(scorer="variance")
fs.fit(X)

VarianceRanking (Deprecated)

Compatibility wrapper.

Equivalent to:

RankingFilter(scorer="variance")

Included for backward compatibility only.

Score-Based Methods

Score-based methods compute a relevance score and then convert it to a ranking.

ScoreFilter

Base class for score-to-ranking conversion.

Primarily intended for: - scoring functions producing continuous values - methods wishing to override compute_scores()

Custom usage example:

class MyScorer(ScoreFilter):
    def compute_scores(self, X, y):
        return X.var(axis=0)

fs = MyScorer()
fs.fit(X, y)

API Reference

Public API for fsmethods.

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.CorrelationScore(method='pearson', name=None, target_type='regression')[source]

Bases: FSMethod, BaseEstimator

Absolute correlation score per feature with target (Pearson or Spearman).

Parameters:

  • method (str)

  • name (str)

  • target_type (str)

fit(X, y)[source]
Return type:

CorrelationScore

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.FSMethod(name=None, target_type=None)[source]

Bases: ABC, BaseEstimator

Abstract base class for all feature selection methods. Inherits from sklearn’s BaseEstimator to support clone/get_params/set_params.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • target_type (str)

abstractmethod fit(X, y)[source]
Return type:

FSMethod

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray)

get_feature_importances()[source]
Return type:

ndarray

get_ranking()[source]
Return type:

ndarray

get_support(indices=False)[source]

Get a mask, or integer index, of the features selected.

If selected_features_ is None (e.g., score/rank methods), infer a mask of all True using the length of feature_importances_ or ranking_.

Return type:

Union[ndarray, List[str]]

Parameters:

indices (bool)

transform(X, threshold=None, n_features=None)[source]

Reduce X to the selected features.

Selection priority: 1) If n_features is provided and ranking_ exists -> take top-k by ranking_. 2) Else if threshold is provided and feature_importances_ exists -> score >= threshold. 3) Else -> use get_support() (selected_features_ or inferred “all True”).

Return type:

Union[DataFrame, ndarray]

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • threshold (float | None)

  • n_features (int | None)

Notes

  • This keeps backward-compatibility for subset methods while making score/rank

methods usable without precomputing a binary mask.

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.FisherScore(name=None, target_type='classification')[source]

Bases: FSMethod, BaseEstimator

Fisher score for classification: ratio of between-class to within-class variance, per feature.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • target_type (str)

fit(X, y)[source]
Return type:

FisherScore

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.MIScore(target_type='classification', discrete_features='auto', n_neighbors=3, name=None, random_state=None)[source]

Bases: FSMethod, BaseEstimator

Mutual Information score per feature (classification or regression).

Parameters:

  • target_type (str)

  • discrete_features (Union[bool, str])

  • n_neighbors (int)

  • name (str)

  • random_state (Optional[int])

fit(X, y)[source]
Return type:

MIScore

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.RankingFilter(scorer='anova', target_type='classification', name=None)[source]

Bases: FSMethod, BaseEstimator

Unified ranking filter with multiple scorers.

Parameters:

  • scorer ({"anova","ttest","mi","chi2","pearson","spearman","variance"}) – Underlying univariate scorer. Higher is better.

  • target_type ({"classification","regression"}) – Used to choose MI variant and validate scenarios.

  • name (Optional[str])

fit(X, y)[source]
Return type:

RankingFilter

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray)

class pyensemblefs.fsmethods.SubsetFilter(rule='variance', k=None, threshold=None, name=None, target_type=None)[source]

Bases: FSMethod, BaseEstimator

Parameters:

  • rule (str)

  • k (Optional[int])

  • threshold (Optional[float])

  • name (Optional[str])

  • target_type (Optional[str])

fit(X, y=None)[source]
Return type:

SubsetFilter

Parameters:

  • X (pandas.DataFrame | ndarray)

  • y (pandas.Series | ndarray | None)

pyensemblefs.fsmethods.make_fs(kind, name, **kwargs)[source]
Return type:

Any

Parameters:

  • kind (str)

  • name (str)

pyensemblefs.fsmethods.register_fs(kind, name, ctor)[source]
Return type:

None

Parameters:

  • kind (str)

  • name (str)

  • ctor (Callable[[...], Any])
